events.event: Germany invades Poland, leading to the UK's declaration of war two days later.
The death of Pope Adrian IV, the only Englishman to have been Pope.
The birth of Hakeem Kae-Kazim, the British-Nigerian actor.
The Great Fire of London begins at Thomas Farriner's bakery in Pudding Lane.
The official end of WWII with the formal surrender of Japan.
The birth of Lennox Lewis, the British heavyweight boxing champion.
The United Kingdom declares war on Germany following the invasion of Poland.
The death of Oliver Cromwell, Lord Protector of England.
The UK adopts the Gregorian calendar; eleven days are 'lost' as Sept 2 is followed by Sept 14.
The death of George Robey, the 'Prime Minister of Mirth' and music hall star.
The birth of Beyoncé; the artist's concerts and tours are major events in the UK.
The birth of Jennie Jerome, later Lady Randolph Churchill and mother of Winston Churchill.
The birth of Freddie Mercury, the lead singer of Queen, in Zanzibar.
The Munich Olympics massacre; the tragedy had a profound impact on UK security policy.
The launch of Voyager 1; British scientists have been integral to the mission's data analysis.
The funeral of Diana, Princess of Wales, is held at Westminster Abbey.
The birth of Idris Elba, the acclaimed English actor and musician.
The Victoria returns to Spain, completing the first circumnavigation of the globe; the feat inspired later British explorers.
The birth of Queen Elizabeth I at Greenwich Palace.
Grace Darling and her father rescue survivors from the wrecked Forfarshire off the Farne Islands.
The death of the thylacine (Tasmanian tiger); its extinction is a key focus of UK conservation study.
The death of Queen Elizabeth II at Balmoral Castle, marking the end of the longest reign in British history.
The birth of Richard I (the Lionheart) in Oxford.
The birth of Martin Freeman, the English actor famous for 'The Hobbit' and 'Sherlock'.
The death of William the Conqueror in France.
The Battle of Flodden: English forces defeat the Scots, and James IV is killed.
The birth of Hugh Grant, the celebrated English actor.
The birth of Jose Feliciano; the artist's work has been consistently popular in the UK.
The birth of Guy Ritchie, the English film director and screenwriter.
George Smith becomes the first person to be convicted of drink-driving in London.
The Battle of Stirling Bridge: William Wallace defeats the English army.
The 9/11 attacks in the USA; the events triggered the UK's involvement in the 'War on Terror'.
The birth of Harry Connick Jr.; his music is a staple of UK jazz radio.
The liberation of Brussels; British forces were key to the city's freedom.
The birth of Rachel Ward, the English-Australian actress and director.
The birth of Richard March Hoe; his printing technology revolutionised the British newspaper industry.
The birth of Roald Dahl, the world-famous Welsh author of children's books.
The Battle of the Plains of Abraham: British forces under Wolfe defeat the French in Quebec.
The birth of Jacqueline Bisset, the acclaimed English actress.
The British Empire adopts the Gregorian calendar (day after Sept 2).
The death of Arthur Wellesley, 1st Duke of Wellington.
The birth of Amy Winehouse, the influential English singer-songwriter.
Battle of Britain Day: The RAF defeats a major Luftwaffe attack, a turning point in the air war.
The birth of Agatha Christie, the 'Queen of Crime' novelist.
The birth of Prince Harry, Duke of Sussex, at St Mary's Hospital, London.
Owain Glyndŵr is proclaimed Prince of Wales by his followers.
The death of Marc Bolan, the lead singer of T. Rex and a glam rock icon.
The birth of Richard Marx; the artist has had numerous hits on the UK charts.
Operation Market Garden begins, a major Allied airborne operation involving British divisions.
The birth of Damon Hill, the English racing driver and Formula One world champion.
The US Constitution is signed; its principles were deeply rooted in English common law.
The Scottish Independence Referendum is held, with a majority voting to remain in the UK.
The death of Jimi Hendrix in London.
The birth of Samuel Johnson, the creator of the influential English dictionary.
The Battle of Poitiers: English forces under the Black Prince defeat the French and capture King John II.
The birth of Jeremy Irons, the acclaimed English actor.
The birth of David Soul; the actor's work in 'Starsky & Hutch' was a UK TV mainstay.
The Battle of Alma: British and French forces defeat the Russians during the Crimean War.
The birth of George R.R. Martin; his work has had immense cultural impact in the UK.
The birth of Sophia Loren; the actress is a fixture of international cinema in the UK.
The Battle of Prestonpans: Jacobite forces under Bonnie Prince Charlie defeat the government army.
The birth of H.G. Wells, the pioneering British science fiction author.
The birth of Liam Gallagher, the lead singer of Oasis.
Sir Robert Walpole becomes the first person to move into 10 Downing Street.
ITV, the first independent television network in the UK, begins broadcasting.
The Deal barracks bombing by the IRA kills 11 bandsmen of the Royal Marines.
The discovery of Neptune; British astronomer John Couch Adams had independently predicted its position.
The birth of Bruce Springsteen; his UK tours are major cultural events.
The birth of Kublai Khan; his life became legendary in British historical literature.
The birth of Phil Hartman; the comedian's work reached the UK via 'The Simpsons' and SNL.
The first flight of a steam-powered airship; the technology was closely followed by British engineers.
The birth of Nia Vardalos; her films have been popular in UK cinemas.
The Battle of Stamford Bridge: Harold Godwinson defeats the Vikings, ending the Viking Age in England.
The end of the Battle of Arnhem during Operation Market Garden.
The birth of Catherine Zeta-Jones, the acclaimed Welsh actress.
The first televised presidential debate (Nixon vs Kennedy); the format was later adopted in the UK.
Stanislav Petrov prevents a nuclear war; the event was a key focus of UK Cold War studies.
The birth of Olivia Newton-John, the English-Australian singer and actress.
The Stockton and Darlington Railway, the world's first public steam-powered railway, opens.
The birth of Gwyneth Paltrow; the actress's work is widely popular in the UK.
The birth of Meat Loaf; his albums have had massive success in the UK.
William the Conqueror lands at Pevensey, beginning the Norman Conquest.
Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin at St Mary's Hospital, London.
The birth of Naomi Watts, the acclaimed English-Australian actress.
The birth of Horatio Nelson at Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk.
The Metropolitan Police Service begins its first patrols in London.
The birth of Sebastian Coe, the British middle-distance runner and Olympic organiser.
Neville Chamberlain returns from Munich and delivers his 'Peace for our time' speech.
Henry IV is proclaimed King of England after the deposition of Richard II.
The death of James Dawson; though American, his poetry was studied in the UK.
